items.title: Top Glove's Q3 net profit up 1.6pct to RM30.76mil on RM1.0bil revenue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Top Glove Corp Bhd's third-quarter net profit grew 1.6 per cent to RM30.76 million from RM30.28 million a year ago, driven by higher sales orders across most regions. Revenue for the quarter ended Feb 28, 2026 also rose 14 per cent to RM1 billion from RM883.65 million last year, according to a bourse filing. Top Glove said the Middle East conflict has given rise to global crude oil supply disruption, which affects the availability of nitrile latex, a key raw material for nitrile gloves.

items.title: Motorists urged to prioritise safety during festive exodus
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: As Malaysians prepared for the annual festive exodus, drivers are urged to plan their journeys carefully and exercise patience to ensure the safety of themselves, their passengers and other road users, the Road Safety Expert Association (RSEA) said. "Patience while driving is also crucial for road safety. Jamil said fatigue and drowsiness were among the main contributors to road accidents, especially during festive seasons.

items.title: Umbrellas up: MetMalaysia sounds storm warning for KL, Selangor, eight states
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a fresh weather alert for several regions across the country, with heavy rain, thunderstorms, and strong winds expected to persist until 5pm today. According to the advisory issued at 2.15pm, the affected areas in Peninsular Malaysia include the entire Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur as well as the Selangor districts of Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, and Petaling. In East Malaysia, the weather warning covers Sri Aman, Kapit (Song and Kapit), and Miri (Telang Usan) in Sarawak.

items.title: Beware litterbugs, SWCorp goes undercover on LTU expressway
items.summary: KUANTAN: Plainclothes enforcement officers have been deployed along certain stretches of the Lingkaran Tengah Utama (LTU) Expressway to nab litterbugs during the upcoming Aidilfitri holiday. Solid Waste and Public Cleansing Management Corporation (SWCorp) chief executive officer Khalid Mohamed said that by blending in with regular traffic, these officers would monitor road-users along the toll-free dual carriageway, which has been singled out as a littering hotspot. "Enforcement officers are going undercover to maintain cleanliness in public areas, including highways and Rest and Service Areas.

items.title: Indonesian man charged with causing teen’s death while carrying sickle in car
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — An Indonesian man was charged in the Teluk Intan Magistrate's Court today with two counts of dangerous driving, including one for causing the death of a teenager last week while allegedly carrying a long-handled sickle. The accused, Sutiyono, 39, who is hearing-impaired, nodded to show he understood the charges read before Magistrate Naidatul Athirah Azman, Berita Harian reported. For the first and more serious charge, Sutiyono is accused of causing the death of 18-year-old Adham Faisal through dangerous driving.

items.title: [UPDATED] Syndicate uses cloned identities, alters chassis numbers to mask stolen vehicles
items.summary: Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar said forensic examinations conducted on seized vehicles revealed extensive modifications aimed at concealing their true identities. Also present were Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) deputy director (intelligence and operations) Datuk Mohd Sukri Kaman and Criminal Investigation Department (CID) D4 division principal assistant director Senior Assistant Commissioner Noor Dellhan Yahaya. He said investigators were able to recover two original chassis numbers through forensic processes.

items.title: #GrabBersamaMalaysia provides community support during Ramadan
items.summary: GRAB Malaysia is reinforcing its connection with communities across the country this Ramadan through a series of initiatives under the #GrabBersamaMalaysia campaign. The effort brings together employees, driver- and delivery-partners, merchant-partners and residents to restore community spaces, share meals, and support families, reflecting the values of patience and gratitude. SURAU RESTORATION IN HULU LANGAT Grab Malaysia joined forces with GXBank, Jaya Grocer and Lembaga Zakat Selangor (LZS) to restore Surau Nur Ramadhan in Hulu Langat, a vital community space in Kampung Paya Seberang that had fallen into disrepair.

items.title: US military says hit Iran missile sites near Strait with 'bunker buster' bombs
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US military said Tuesday it had hit Iranian missile sites near the strategic Strait of Hormuz with some of the most powerful bombs in the US arsenal. "US forces successfully employed multiple 5,000-pound deep penetrator munitions on hardened Iranian missile sites along Iran's coastline near the Strait of Hormuz," Central Command said in a statement on X. "The Iranian anti-ship cruise missiles in these sites posed a risk to international shipping in the strait."

items.title: Tabung Haji posts highest profit in eight years, 3.5pc payout to benefit 9.7 million depositors
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The Pilgrims’ Fund Board (Tabung Haji -TH) today announced a profit distribution of 3.50 per cent, after zakat, for the 2025 financial year, the highest rate recorded since 2018. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs), Zulkifli Hasan, said the total profit distribution amounted to RM3.22 billion, benefiting more than 9.7 million TH depositors. Also present were TH Group Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer Mustakim Mohamad, TH Chairman Abdul Rashid Hussain, and TH Finance Department Group Executive Director Shamsul Bahar Shamsudin.

items.title: Iran strikes Tel Aviv with cluster warheads in retaliation for killing of security chief
items.summary: DUBAI/TEL AVIV: Iran targeted Tel Aviv with missiles carrying cluster warheads in what it said was retaliation for Israel's assassination of Iran's security chief Ali Larijani, Iranian state television reported on Wednesday. A statement by Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said weapons used included Khorramshahr 4 and Qadr missiles, both with multi-warheads. The Iranian government on Tuesday confirmed the killing of Larijani, the most senior figure targeted since the US-Israeli war's first day, when an Israeli strike killed Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

items.title: Penang police: Baby found unconscious at Seberang Jaya daycare died of asphyxiation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — A four-month-old baby boy who died while under the care of a daycare centre near Seberang Jaya Hospital last week was asphyxiated, police confirmed today based on initial autopsy findings. Penang Police Chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said investigation papers are nearly complete and will soon be referred to the Deputy Public Prosecutor for further action, Sinar Harian reported. The victim, identified as Hayl Haarith Mohd Syafaeez, was found unconscious at the 24-hour daycare, located within the hospital compound, in the early hours of last Monday.

items.title: RTD deploys 245 officers to monitor accident-prone areas during Hari Raya
items.summary: Penang RTD director Zulkifly Ismail said enforcement is conducted for 16 hours daily, and 24 hours during full movement restrictions across five districts — Seberang Perai Utara (SPU), Seberang Perai Tengah (SPT), Seberang Perai Selatan (SPS), Timur Laut and Barat Daya. "We identified eight frequent accident areas on the mainland, namely Jalan Teluk Air Tawar to Jalan Bagan Luar, the North-South Expressway in SPU, Jalan Perusahaan Perai in SPT, and Jalan Besar Simpang Ampat in SPS. "On the island, the locations included the Tun Dr Lim Chong Eu Expressway, Jalan Paya Terubong in Timur Laut, as well as Jalan Tun Dr Awang and Jalan Sultan Azlan Shah in Barat Daya," he said.

items.title: Businesses need ready cash relief, wait-and-see approach could hurt MSMEs if Gulf crisis deepens, employers tells govt
items.summary: President Datuk Dr Syed Hussain Syed Husman, while acknowledging the government’s cautious approach, called for preparedness and flexibility in policy response should the situation in West Asia deteriorate. However, the government must remain ready to act swiftly if conditions worsen,” he said in a statement today. Syed Hussain said the situation reinforces the need for businesses to accelerate productivity enhancements, diversify supply chains, and adopt technology to mitigate future disruptions.

items.title: Taxpayers need not amend 2025 returns over employer tax marking errors
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Individual taxpayers are not required to file an amended Income Tax Return Form (BNCP) for the 2025 Year of Assessment if they made an inadvertent error in the 'Tax Borne by Employer' section. For cases confirmed as marking errors, excess tax refunds will be processed in stages. "The IRB noted that some taxpayers mistakenly marked the 'Tax Borne by Employer' section when completing their Income Tax Return Forms for the 2025 Year of Assessment, resulting in delays in receiving excess tax refunds.

items.title: Australia's Rest to invest up to US$250mil in US retail property fund
items.summary: SYDNEY: Australian pension fund Rest said on Wednesday it would invest up to US$250 million in a US retail property fund, as it banks on traditional retailing remaining strong across America. The investment will be made to the US Cities Retail Fund (USCRF), managed by Nuveen Real Estate, which owns neighbourhood shopping centres across the country's major metropolitan areas. Rest said the centres were tenanted by major US grocery stores and retailers such as Trader Joe's, The Fresh Market and Harris Teeter.

items.title: Mother allowed to join judicial review over daughter's death inquest
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The High Court here has allowed an application by Noraidah Lamat to be joined as an interested party in a judicial review. A non-governmental organisation, who filed the judicial review, sought from the court to quash the inquest into Noraidah's daughter Zara Qairina Mahathir's death. High Court judge Datuk Celestina Stuel Galid made the decision after considering written submissions from both Noraidah and Pertubuhan Kebajikan Ekonomi Semangat Bersatu Malaysia (Pembela), as well as oral clarifications from Pembela.

items.title: 1,054 litterbugs caught by SWCorp since Jan 1
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Solid Waste and Public Cleansing Management Corporation (SWCorp) has detained 1,054 litterbugs nationwide since Jan 1. "Many have already started serving community service sentences. Nga said that the majority of those committing minor littering offences were caught throwing cigarette butts.

items.title: Driver's delay at petrol station sparks viral frustration ahead of Hari Raya [WATCH]
items.summary: Where is the common sense?" She added: "Help, I want to go back for Hari Raya, please have common sense! People like this really have no common sense."

items.title: JKR projects see mixed progress, Kuching complex leads the way with one-third on track
items.summary: KUCHING, March 18 — About one-third of Public Works Department (JKR) projects nationwide are currently progressing ahead of schedule, said Deputy Works Minister Ahmad Maslan. He said that of the 855 ongoing projects, roughly one-third are either ahead of time or on track, while the remainder are behind schedule. Those that are behind schedule must be prioritised,” he said after inspecting the Kuching Federal Multipurpose Building Complex (BGP) construction site today.

items.title: Crest Builder unit secures RM513mil new jobs, boosting order book to RM2bil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Crest Builder Holdings Bhd's wholly-owned subsidiary, Crest Builder Sdn Bhd (CBSB), has secured two Letters of Award (LoA) with a combined value of RM513 million. The first, valued at RM246.4 million, covers construction of the East One SJCC development in Subang Jaya, Selangor, for Sime Darby Property (Subang) Sdn Bhd, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Sime Darby Property Bhd. The second LoA, worth RM266.5 million, is from Freedom Estates Sdn Bhd, a subsidiary of BRDB Developments Group, for the Miranda Hill development in Mont Kiara, Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: [UPDATED] Police warn buyers against 'too good to be true' car deals amid cloning syndicate bust
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have urged the public to exercise caution when purchasing used vehicles, warning that attractive deals could be linked to criminal syndicates involved in vehicle cloning. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar said buyers should be wary of prices that were significantly lower than current market value. Also present were Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) deputy director (intelligence and operations) Datuk Mohd Sukri Kaman and Criminal Investigation Department (CID) D4 division principal assistant director Senior Assistant Commissioner Noor Dellhan Yahaya.

items.title: Sarawak government aligns with Putrajaya, declares additional Hari Raya holiday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The Sarawak government has officially confirmed that it will observe the additional public holiday for Hari Raya Aidilfitri 2026, aligning with the recent announcement made by the federal government. The statement clarified that if Hari Raya falls on Friday, March 20, the additional holiday will be observed on the following Monday, March 23. However, if the first day of Syawal is on Saturday, March 21, then the preceding Friday, March 20, will be the public holiday.

items.title: [UPDATED] Police bust vehicle cloning syndicate, seize RM1.1mil worth of cars
items.summary: Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar said the operation, dubbed Op Lejang Mewah Klon, was the result of nearly five months of intelligence gathering. Police also detained a local man wanted by the Singapore Police Force (SPF) in connection with vehicle theft cases in the republic. Among the vehicles confiscated were Toyota Vellfire, Toyota Estima, Toyota Altis, Toyota Harrier, Mazda 3, Honda City, Honda City Hatchback and Toyota Camry.

items.title: Murder charge for Melaka man over fatal attack on associate in Sungai Udang, case set for April 29
items.summary: MELAKA, March 18 — A man was charged at the Ayer Keroh Magistrate’s Court here today with murdering his acquaintance. Saiful Hadi Othman, 44, is charged under Section 302 of the Penal Code with the murder of Muhamad Hanafi Yusof, 25, at a house in Sungai Udang here, between 7.58 pm and 8.15 pm on March 11 this year. Deputy Public Prosecutor Syaza Nur Sharif prosecuted, while Saiful Hadi was unrepresented.

items.title: Najib moves to reinstate RM1.9mil suit against Tommy Thomas
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Datuk Seri Najib Razak has filed a motion seeking leave to appeal to the Federal Court to reinstate his RM1.9 million civil lawsuit against former attorney-general Tan Sri Tommy Thomas. The application, filed on Feb 10, follows a Court of Appeal decision that unanimously upheld a 2022 Kuala Lumpur High Court ruling dismissing the former prime minister's suit. Najib, 72, is seeking to appeal based on three legal questions regarding his claim of misfeasance in public office arising from his prosecution in the 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) case.

items.title: Police bust vehicle cloning syndicate, seize RM1.1mil worth of cars
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have crippled a vehicle theft and cloning syndicate operating across several states with the arrest of 12 individuals and the seizure of vehicles worth an estimated RM1.1 million. The arrests were made during a coordinated operation conducted by Bukit Aman's Criminal Investigation Department (CID) D4 division, together with contingent CID teams, between March 12 and March 15. CID director Datuk M. Kumar said the operation, dubbed Op Lejang Mewah Klon, was the result of nearly five months of intelligence gathering.

items.title: Race against the clock: Indira Gandhi launches contempt action against IGP before missing daughter turns 18
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — In a final and desperate bid to recover her long-abducted daughter, M. Indira Gandhi has initiated contempt of court proceedings against the Inspector-General of Police for the force’s decade-long failure to execute a court order to locate the child. The legal move comes with immense urgency, as Indira’s daughter, Prasana Diksa, is set to turn 18 early next month. Indira’s lawyer, Rajesh Nagarajan, said the action was taken due to the “lackadaisical attitude” of the police, Free Malaysia Today reported.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan MB says following Putrajaya’s lead, cancels state Raya open house to save cost
items.summary: SEREMBAN, March 18 — The Negeri Sembilan state government has cancelled its Aidilfitri open house this year in line with a federal decision, as part of cost-saving measures. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said the event scheduled to take place in Bandar Sri Jempol on April 4, as well as the national-level Madani Aidilfitri celebration on April 12, will not proceed. He said the decision reflects a prudent approach to managing the state’s finances.

items.title: Control over domestic policy, regional harmonisation, will ensure medicine security
items.summary: LETTERS: The escalating geopolitical conflict involving Iran in the Middle East has triggered a surge in global oil prices and freight costs. For Malaysia, which imported RM8.22 billion worth of finished pharmaceutical products in 2025, our dependency on imports brings the conversation surrounding medicine security back into focus. Second, we must focus on building local manufacturing capacity.

items.title: Police suspect asphyxiation in baby's death at childcare centre
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: A four-month-old boy who died after being found unresponsive at a 24-hour childcare centre is believed to have succumbed to asphyxia. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said a medical examination indicated this as the cause of death. On March 9, Hayl Haarith Mohd Syafaeez was found unresponsive at the centre, located within the Seberang Jaya Hospital grounds.

items.title: Late Zayn Rayyan's father seeks RM65k public aid for court costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The father of the late Zayn Rayyan Abdul Matin, Zaim Ikhwan Zahari, today appealed to the public for assistance to cover legal costs for himself and his wife, Ismanira Abdul Manaf, at the High Court. Zaim said that since the case began at the Sessions Court, they had already spent RM200,000 on legal fees and related court expenses. Zaim added that the court costs were extremely high and beyond his means.

items.title: Ex-HR minister Steven Sim touts pro-worker record after High Court rules he was ‘biased’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR — Former human resources minister Steven Sim has defended his pro-worker record, following a High Court ruling that found he acted with bias in favour of employers during a trade dispute last year. Sim reiterated his position, saying his track record in government has always been to support workers, Free Malaysia Today reported. When asked about the ruling, Sim, declined to comment due to the ongoing legal process.

items.title: PN's East Coast strength insufficient to make Ahmad Samsuri prime minister
items.summary: Pas treasurer Datuk Iskandar Abdul Samad said that even if PN were to win all the seats in the areas, it would still not be enough to appoint PN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar as prime minister. "Dr Sam (Samsuri), as PN chairman, is seen by many as a potential candidate for the country's 11th prime minister. "It cannot be denied that PN, especially Pas, is strong in the East Coast.

items.title: British Airway's London-Melbourne route boost KLIA as Asia-Pacific hub
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: British Airways will introduce a daily London to Melbourne service via Kuala Lumpur starting Jan 9, 2027, further cementing Kuala Lumpur International Airport's (KLIA) status as a strategic transit hub connecting Europe, Southeast Asia, and Australia, Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) said. MAHB managing director, Datuk Mohd Izani Ghani, said the new route reflects strong confidence in KLIA as a leading Southeast Asian gateway. "The service enhances connectivity across three key regions, Europe, Southeast Asia and Australia, while strengthening Kuala Lumpur's role as an efficient transit hub for long-haul travel.

items.title: Indira files committal application against office of IGP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: M. Indira Gandhi has filed an application for committal action against the office of the Inspector-General of Police for contempt of court, accusing the police of failing to arrest her ex-husband and locate her abducted daughter for over a decade. The move stems from a 2014 Ipoh High Court recovery order which compelled police to find Indira's daughter, Prasana Diksa, and arrest her ex-husband, Muhammad Riduan Abdullah. Indira's lawyer, Rajesh Nagarajan, told the New Straits Times that despite many years, the court order has yet to be excuted.

items.title: 1.5 million vehicles expected to enter Penang during Hari Raya
items.summary: State police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the projection covered the duration of Op Selamat 26, which was conducted from March 19 to March 24. Azizee said the operation was aimed at ensuring road users arrived safely at their destinations while homes left behind remained secure under police monitoring. Meanwhile, Azizee said police had identified several accident-prone hotspots and blackspots across the state, including Jalan Teluk Air Tawar, Jalan Perusahaan Prai, Jalan Besar Simpang Empat (Km12 to Km32), Lebuhraya Tun Dr Lim Chong Eu, Jalan Air Itam, Jalan Sultan Azlan Shah, Jalan Tun Dr Awang and Butterworth Outer Ring Road (BORR).

items.title: Budget hotels not expecting increase in bookings for Hari Raya
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Budget hotels across Malaysia are facing a challenging Hari Raya season, with high cost of living, stagnant room rates and unlicensed accommodations that continue to affect their profit margins. Malaysia Budget and Business Hotel Association (MyBHA) president Dr Sri Ganesh Michiel said the traditional "balik kampung" rush no longer guarantees an immediate windfall for the budget hotel sector. Ganesh said there is a noticeable trend in booking patterns, where budget hotels are often the secondary choice for families traveling back to their hometowns.

items.title: Does the AG have immunity? Najib’s launches final bid to revive suit against Tommy Thomas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak has taken his RM1.9 million lawsuit against former attorney general Tan Sri Tommy Thomas to the Federal Court in a final bid to revive the case. The move comes after the Court of Appeal in January upheld a High Court decision to strike out the suit, which alleges misfeasance in public office and malicious prosecution, Free Malaysia Today reported Najib sued Thomas in 2020 over charges brought against him related to 1MDB, seeking RM1.9 million in special damages to cover his legal defence preparation costs. In a significant finding, however, the appellate court disagreed with the High Court on one key point: the immunity of the attorney general.

items.title: TNB steps up preparations for Aidilfitri power demand
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) has made comprehensive preparations to ensure a stable and continuous electricity supply during this year's Aidilfitri celebrations. As part of its readiness plan, TNB has implemented several electricity network upgrade projects nationwide, while continuously monitoring its network and critical assets. As a further mitigation measure, TNB has also carried out load balancing initiatives, increased transformer capacity and enhanced its electricity network system to ensure stable distribution throughout the festive period.

items.title: Indonesian man charged over student's death in sickle-linked crash
items.summary: TELUK INTAN: An Indonesian man was charged in the magistrate's court here today with two offences for riding a motorcycle dangerously, resulting in the death of a vocational college student and injuring another teenager in an incident last week. According to the first charge, Sutiyono, who was riding a Honda EX5 motorcycle, was accused of riding in a manner dangerous to the public, causing the death of 18-year-old Adham Faisal. For the second charge, Sutiyono was accused of riding dangerously, causing injury to 17-year-old Muhammad Mu'ammar Ismail at the same place, date and time.

items.title: Tabung Haji declares 3.5pct payout for FY25, highest in eight years
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) has declared a higher profit distribution of 3.50 per cent after zakat for the financial year ended Dec 31, 2025 (FY25), compared with 3.25 per cent in FY24, marking its highest payout in eight years. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan said the distribution involves RM3.22 billion and will benefit more than 9.7 million TH depositors. TH declared zakat of 2.58 per cent for FY25, bringing total zakat payments to state Islamic religious councils on behalf of depositors to RM95.3 million.

items.title: Ringgit climbs to five-year high vs Singapore dollar amid Middle East tensions [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit climbed to the strongest versus the Singapore dollar in five years amid cautious sentiment surrounding the Middle East conflict. On Wednesday, the local currency strengthened 0.2 per cent to 3.0615 versus its Singaporean counterpart, a level last seen in March 2021. As at 9am, the ringgit further strengthened to 3.0605 against the Singapore dollar.

items.title: Free Go KL bus to Cheras Rehab Hospital from April 1, says Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Federal Territories Minister Hannah Yeoh today announced that the free Go KL bus service will be extended to the Cheras Rehabilitation Hospital (HRC) from April 1, in a move aimed at improving access to public healthcare, particularly for the elderly and those with mobility challenges. She said Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) will expand the GOKL11 route – which runs between MRT Maluri and Bandar Tun Razak – by adding a new stop within the hospital grounds. She said the bus service will provide much needed access to the public.

items.title: Penang JPJ ramps up Raya patrols in road safety push, aims to cut accidents by 10pc
items.summary: BATU KAWAN, March 18 — The Penang Road Transport Department (JPJ) is intensifying its advocacy programmes during Ops Hari Raya Aidilfitri (HRA) 2026, aiming to reduce road accidents by 10 per cent throughout the festive season. State JPJ director Zulkifly Ismail said more face-to-face engagements with drivers are being carried out to boost the Perception of Being Caught (POBC) among road users. He said eight key areas have been identified as frequent accident zones, including Jalan Teluk Air Tawar to Jalan Bagan Luar and the North-South Expressway in North Seberang Perai; Jalan Perusahaan Perai in Central Seberang Perai; Jalan Besar Simpang Ampat in South Seberang Perai; Tun Dr Lim Chong Eu Expressway and Jalan Paya Terubong in the Northeast; as well as Jalan Tun Dr Awang and Jalan Sultan Azlan Shah in the Southwest.

items.title: Tick-tock trouble: Domestic trade officers seize knock-off luxe watches worth nearly RM800,000 in Putrajaya
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 18 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) seized 790 units of counterfeit branded watches, worth an estimated RM790,600, during a raid in the federal capital on March 13. KPDN Enforcement director-general Datuk Azman Adam said the raid was conducted by the Putrajaya Enforcement Division following a nearly month-long investigation into premises suspected of storing and distributing the fake watches. Azman said the counterfeit watches were believed to have been sourced from overseas suppliers before being sold to local customers.

items.title: Anwar wins bid to strike out legal challenge over PM and Tambun MP posts, judge rules Waytha Moorthy has no case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The High Court here today allowed Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s bid to strike out an originating summons filed by P. Waytha Moorthy challenging his appointment as Prime Minister and Tambun Member of Parliament (MP). Judge Alice Loke Yee Ching, in allowing the application, held that the court has no jurisdiction to entertain the summons, as any challenge to an election must be brought by way of an election petition. Justice Loke further said that the court, in hearing the originating summons, was not sitting as an election court, nor was the action commenced by way of an election petition.

items.title: Hire-purchase changes aim to protect borrowers, little impact on banks, says economist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The introduction of key amendments to hire-purchase financing practices under the Hire-Purchase (Amendment) Act 2026 (HPAA) marks a significant move in strengthening borrowers’ protection, particularly for those looking to sell vehicles before their financing contracts mature, an economist said. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the changes would benefit borrowers who intend to trade in or dispose of their vehicles early, as the reducing balance method offers greater savings than the Rule of 78, which front-loads interest at the beginning of the loan tenure. He said to some degree, it could help improve purchasers’ affordability.

items.title: Accidental tick? Marking error won’t hold up 2025 income tax refunds, IRB assures taxpayers
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 18 — Individual taxpayers do not need to submit an amended Income Tax Return Form (BNCP) for the Year of Assessment 2025 to correct an accidental error in marking the “Tax Borne by Employer” column. “The IRB has noted that some individual taxpayers inadvertently marked the ‘Tax borne by employer’ column when filling out their Income Tax Return Forms for the Year of Assessment 2025, resulting in delays in receiving excess tax refunds. “For cases confirmed as marking errors, the excess tax refunds will be processed in stages,” the statement said The IRB explained that the tax refund system operates automatically based on the information provided by taxpayers in their returns.

items.title: ‘Balik kampung’ traffic from Klang Valley: East coast-bound jams start early
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Traffic from the Klang Valley heading to the east coast began to build up this morning as travellers started returning to their hometowns ahead of Aidilfitri. “LLM expects traffic volume to increase further from midday following the 50 per cent toll discount, which began at midnight and will run until 11.59 pm tomorrow,” he told Bernama when contacted. — Bernama

items.title: Nightmare in Kampung Segaliud: Father helpless as crocodile drags son away in Kinabatangan, search ongoing
items.summary: Kinabatangan Fire and Rescue Station chief Hairi Otuh said Mohd Rayyan Abdullah, 28, was attacked while casting a net for prawns in the river. Hairi said the rescue team arrived at the location at 9.10 pm to gather information and immediately launched a search and rescue operation. — Bernama

items.title: Works Ministry’s Raya Gift: A complete AwAS camera cheat sheet (for responsible driving)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The Ministry of Works has issued a public reminder to motorists travelling for the Hari Raya Aidilfitri ‘balik kampung’ exodus, releasing a list of Automated Awareness Safety System (AwAS) camera locations along the North-South Expressway (PLUS). Below are the official AwAS camera locations for motorists’ reference. AwAS camera locations on PLUS Expressway Northbound: KM 1, Johor Bahru KM 151.4, Pagoh KM 184.2, Jasin KM 214.4, Alor Gajah KM 299.9, Kampar KM 301.6, Kajang KM 375.9, Slim River KM 204.6, Taiping KM 174, Bandar Baharu KM 97.2, Kuala Muda Southbound: KM 96.3, Kuala Muda KM 166, Seberang Perai Selatan KM 214.4, Alor Gajah KM 328.8, Behrang KM 185, Bemban KM 146.8, Pagoh KM 1, Johor Bahru

items.title: Higher jet fuel prices from Gulf conflict could hurt Sabah tourism comeback, industry leader warns
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 18 — Tourism industry leader Datuk Dr Tan Kok Liang warned that the ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran will inevitably disrupt global and domestic travel patterns, specifically threatening Sabah’s tourism sector. Dr Tan also The emphasised that Sabah’s industry, while diversified, remains vulnerable to global shocks. Currently, he said domestic arrivals make up 60 per cent of visitors, followed by China (18 per cent), Asean (11 per cent), South Korea (5 per cent), and the UK/Europe (3 per cent).

items.title: As Petronas heads to Federal Court, state deputy minister says Sarawak ready to file own petition to protect Petros
items.summary: KUCHING, March 18 — The Sarawak government is prepared to file its own petition in the Federal Court to ensure all legal issues surrounding the regulatory framework of Petroleum Sarawak Berhad (Petros) are thoroughly examined, said Deputy Minister in the Sarawak Premier’s Department (Law, MA63 and State-Federal Relations), Datuk Sharifah Hasidah Sayeed Aman Ghazali. Petronas is challenging the legislative competency of the Sarawak State Legislature to enact certain laws and to declare several laws made by the Sarawak State Legislature invalid. Some of the provisions include Sections 7, 7A and 8 of the Sarawak Distribution of Gas Ordinance 2016, Section 3 of the Sarawak Interpretation Ordinance 2005, Section 2 of the Sarawak Oil Mining Ordinance 1958, Section 2 of the Sarawak National Parks and Nature Reserves Ordinance 1998 and Section 2 of the Sarawak Land Code 1958.

items.title: Lim Guan Eng moots tax moratorium, interest-free loans to help MSMEs in ‘crisis of survival’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — DAP advisor Lim Guan Eng today pressed the government to implement urgent measures to help micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) that he said are facing a “crisis of survival” due to the ongoing Middle East conflict. To contain costs and protect businesses, Lim proposed a moratorium on new financial burdens, which would include suspending increases in the Sales and Service Tax (SST), halting the mandatory e-invoicing implementation, and pausing the 2 per cent Employees Provident Fund (EPF) contribution for foreign workers. “The government must realise that MSMEs are facing a real crisis of survival with the ongoing Middle East conflict,” he concluded.

items.title: Man arrested for suspected rape of 14-year-old after ‘khalwat’ raid in Muar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Police have arrested a man in his 20s for the suspected rape of a 14-year-old girl, after the pair were found together in a hotel room during a raid by the Johor Islamic Religious Department (JAINJ) last week. Muar District Police Chief ACP Raizmukhliz Azman Aziz confirmed the arrest in a statement yesterday. “The raid found a man in his 20s together with a girl believed to be approximately 14 years of age inside a hotel room under suspicious circumstances,” Raizmukhliz stated.

items.title: Ahmad Maslan: Umno welcomes back former members while other parties expel theirs
items.summary: KUCHING, March 18 — The ‘Rumah Bangsa’ initiative by Umno is viewed as an extraordinary approach to strengthen the party in the Peninsular and Sabah, said Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan. Meanwhile, Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi had previously said that the ‘Rumah Bangsa’ initiative was open to all without any conditions, including former Umno leaders. Former Umno senior leaders Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein and Khairy Jamaludin are reported to have submitted their application letters to rejoin the party Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dasuki, in a post on Facebook Monday, confirmed that former Umno leaders Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein and Khairy Jamaludin had submitted application letters to rejoin the party.

items.title: ‘Balik kampung’ in Penang? Don’t miss out on free ferry rides this Aidilfitri
items.summary: Penang Port Commission (SPPP) chairman Datuk Yeoh Soon Hin said that the free ferry fare is a manifestation of their ongoing commitment, along with Penang Port Sdn Bhd (PPSB), to facilitate the travel of the people, especially during the festive season. In line with the initiative to provide free ferry services and the 70th anniversary celebration of SPPP (Platinum Jubilee) this year, Yeoh said SPPP is also sponsoring the implementation of community programmes through the “Kids Go Free|Aidilfitri Special” campaign at the Penang Ferry Museum here. He also said the programme allows Malaysian children, aged 12 years old and below, to enjoy free admission to the ferry museum from March 23 to 25.

items.title: Raya scam alert: How Perak ‘kuih’ sellers lost RM300,000 to fake food orders
items.summary: Perak police chief Datuk Noor Hisam Noordin said the suspects’ modus operandi was to initially place orders in certain quantities with bakeries or catering businesses by giving them time to prepare the orders. “After that, the suspects would request additional items, usually ones not produced by the business operator. “Some business operators made payments.

items.title: J-KOM tells Malaysians to brace for economic impact as US-Iran war rages on
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Malaysians must be prepared to adopt a modest lifestyle amid global uncertainty as international conflicts begin to exert pressure on the domestic economy. Community Communications Department (J-KOM) strategic adviser Datuk Dr Budiman Mohd Zohdi said the current situation demands public preparedness, even though these conflicts do not originate from within the country. Budiman said that in dealing with such instability, people need to shift their mindset by managing their personal finances as if they were managing a ‘small government’.

items.title: Six-person Myvi hit by BMW in fatal Kelantan crash, two dead
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, March 18 — Two people were killed in a road accident involving two vehicles at Bandar Baru Tanah Merah along Jalan Machang-Tanah Merah early this morning. Tanah Merah Fire and Rescue Station operations commander Senior Fire Officer Hamdan Abdullah said the victims were a 64-year-old woman and a 14-year-old girl. “Two victims, who were passengers in the Perodua Myvi, died at the scene, while the others survived,” he said when contacted by Bernama today.
